label: Polynesian
license: Creative Commons; see Stellarium Hawaiian starlines sky culture license

constellations.name: The Chief's Fishline
constellations.myth: Manaiakalani, the Chief's Fishline, connects bright stars into the great fishhook associated with Maui. It is a navigational and story-bearing line, turning the sky into a tool for voyaging.

constellations.name: The Backbone
constellations.myth: Ka Iwikuamoo, the Backbone, is a long guiding structure across the sky. Hawaiian starlines emphasize orientation, genealogy, and ocean travel as much as pictured figures.

constellations.name: The Seven
constellations.myth: Na Hiku, the Seven, gathers the familiar seven bright stars of the northern dipper pattern. In this sky culture the group participates in navigation and naming rather than becoming a bear.

constellations.name: The Cat's Cradle
constellations.myth: Ka Hei-Hei o Na Keiki, the Cat's Cradle, uses the Orion stars as a woven game-like figure. The same anchors that form a Greek hunter become a pattern of play and line-work.

constellations.name: Navigator's Triangle
constellations.myth: Navigator's Triangle is a practical oceanic figure. It draws a compact triangle from bright stars and treats the sky as a living compass for movement across the Pacific.
